_comment: ADR-0016 D2: cost is computed from per-thread USAGE (Claude-Platform-sourced,
cache-bucket-aware price table -- there is NO public per-token price-list API
but no pricing field at all), so prices must be pinned somewhere. Anti-drift is
handled by (a) isolating every price in this ONE small, reviewed file, each tier
`--check-pricing-drift` guard, which fails loud when today's date falls outside
every tier's window for a model family.
`cache_multipliers` are fixed and (per ADR-0016 D2's live-confirmed arithmetic,
reproduced exactly by the golden test in tests/test_cost_golden.py) apply
UNIFORMLY across both model families below: a cache-write (creation) token costs
`write_5m`/`write_1h` times the model's OWN base INPUT rate; a cache-read token
costs `read` times that same base input rate. Output happens to price out to
~5x each model's own base input rate on every tier below -- noted as a sanity
observation, NOT stored/derived as a multiplier (output_per_million_usd is
pinned explicitly per tier, not computed, for maximum accuracy per the ADR's
'prices must be pinned, not formula-derived' stance).
